sections.paragraph:
pr. to tread, or trample with the feet (cogn. to כָּבַשׁ; as to the syllable בס, which is primary in this root, see under בּוּס p. 108, A ), hence to wash garments by treading on them when under water. It differs from רָחַץ to wash (the body), as the Gr. λούειν differs from πλύνειν. In Kal it only occurs in Part. כֹּובֵס Isa 7:3 36:2 , a washer of garments, a fuller, Gr. πλυντήρ, κναφεύς, one who cleanses soiled garments, and fulls new ones. See Schneider, Ind.ad Scriptt.Rei Rusticæ, p. 385. Schoöttgen, Trituræ et Fulloniæ Antiquitates, Lips. 1763, 8.

Piel כִּבֵּס and כִּבֶּס-

(1) i.q. Kal Gen 49:11 Exo 19:10. Part. מְכַכֵּס i.q. כֹּבֵס Mal 3:2.

(2) Metaph. to purge the soul from sin, Psal. 51:4 , Psa 51:9 Jer 4:14 but still allusion is made to the original signification of washing, Jer 2:22 Mal 3:2.

Pual, pass. Lev 13:58 15:17 .

Hothpael, pass. הֻכַּבַּס Lev 13:55, 56 Lev 13:56.
